authors_text: Eda Okur,Saurav Sahay,Lama Nachman
title: NLU for Game-based Learning in Real: Initial Evaluations
title_zh: 用于真实游戏学习的 NLU：初步评估
comment: Proceedings of the Games and Natural Language Processing Workshop at LREC 2022
comments: Proceedings of the Games and Natural Language Processing Workshop at LREC 2022
abstract: Intelligent systems designed for play-based interactions should be contextually aware of the users and their surroundings. Spoken Dialogue Systems (SDS) are critical for these interactive agents to carry out effective goal-oriented communication with users in real-time. For the real-world (i.e., in-the-wild) deployment of such conversational agents, improving the Natural Language Understanding (NLU) module of the goal-oriented SDS pipeline is crucial, especially with limited task-specific datasets. This study explores the potential benefits of a recently proposed transformer-based multi-task NLU architecture, mainly to perform Intent Recognition on small-size domain-specific educational game datasets. The evaluation datasets were collected from children practicing basic math concepts via play-based interactions in game-based learning settings. We investigate the NLU performances on the initial proof-of-concept game datasets versus the real-world deployment datasets and observe anticipated performance drops in-the-wild. We have shown that compared to the more straightforward baseline approaches, Dual Intent and Entity Transformer (DIET) architecture is robust enough to handle real-world data to a large extent for the Intent Recognition task on these domain-specific in-the-wild game datasets.

This study explores the potential benefits of a recently proposed transformer-based multi-task NLU architecture, mainly to perform Intent Recognition on small-size domain-specific educational game datasets. The evaluation datasets were collected from children practicing basic math concepts via play-based interactions in game-based learning settings. We investigate the NLU performances on the initial proof-of-concept game datasets versus the real-world deployment datasets and observe anticipated performance drops in-the-wild. We have shown that compared to the more straightforward baseline approaches, Dual Intent and Entity Transformer (DIET) architecture is robust enough to handle real-world data to a large extent for the Intent Recognition task on these domain-specific in-the-wild game datasets.
abstract_zh: 为基于游戏的交互而设计的智能系统应该能够了解用户及其周围环境。语音对话系统（SDS）对于这些交互式代理与用户进行有效的、面向目标的实时沟通至关重要。对于此类对话代理的现实世界（即野外）部署，改进面向目标的 SDS 管道的自然语言理解 (NLU) 模块至关重要，尤其是在特定任务数据集有限的情况下。本研究探讨了最近提出的基于 Transformer 的多任务 NLU 架构的潜在好处，主要是在小型特定领域的教育游戏数据集上执行意图识别。评估数据集是从在基于游戏的学习环境中通过基于游戏的互动练习基本数学概念的儿童收集的。我们研究了初始概念验证游戏数据集与现实世界部署数据集上的 NLU 性能，并观察了实际情况下预期的性能下降。我们已经证明，与更直接的基线方法相比，双重意图和实体转换器（DIET）架构足够强大，可以在很大程度上处理这些特定领域的野外游戏数据集上的意图识别任务的现实世界数据。

AI.tldr: 本文评估了基于Transformer的多任务NLU架构在教育游戏场景下的意图识别性能，特别是在真实世界部署中的表现。
AI.motivation: 为提升游戏化学习中对话系统的上下文感知能力，需解决真实场景下数据有限且环境复杂导致的NLU性能下降问题。
AI.method: 采用Dual Intent and Entity Transformer (DIET)架构，在小型教育游戏数据集和真实部署数据集上进行意图识别实验，并与基线方法对比。
AI.result: DIET架构在真实世界数据集上表现优于传统方法，但相比受控环境存在性能下降，验证了其在领域特定场景中的鲁棒性。
AI.conclusion: 多任务Transformer架构能有效应对真实场景下的数据挑战，为游戏化学习中的NLU系统提供可行解决方案。

authors_text: Jingjun Yi,Beichen Zhou
title: Attention Awareness Multiple Instance Neural Network
title_zh: 注意力意识多实例神经网络
abstract: Multiple instance learning is qualified for many pattern recognition tasks with weakly annotated data. The combination of artificial neural network and multiple instance learning offers an end-to-end solution and has been widely utilized. However, challenges remain in two-folds. Firstly, current MIL pooling operators are usually pre-defined and lack flexibility to mine key instances. Secondly, in current solutions, the bag-level representation can be inaccurate or inaccessible. To this end, we propose an attention awareness multiple instance neural network framework in this paper. It consists of an instance-level classifier, a trainable MIL pooling operator based on spatial attention and a bag-level classification layer. Exhaustive experiments on a series of pattern recognition tasks demonstrate that our framework outperforms many state-of-the-art MIL methods and validates the effectiveness of our proposed attention MIL pooling operators.
summary: Multiple instance learning is qualified for many pattern recognition tasks with weakly annotated data. The combination of artificial neural network and multiple instance learning offers an end-to-end solution and has been widely utilized. However, challenges remain in two-folds. Firstly, current MIL pooling operators are usually pre-defined and lack flexibility to mine key instances. Secondly, in current solutions, the bag-level representation can be inaccurate or inaccessible. To this end, we propose an attention awareness multiple instance neural network framework in this paper. It consists of an instance-level classifier, a trainable MIL pooling operator based on spatial attention and a bag-level classification layer. Exhaustive experiments on a series of pattern recognition tasks demonstrate that our framework outperforms many state-of-the-art MIL methods and validates the effectiveness of our proposed attention MIL pooling operators.
abstract_zh: 多实例学习适用于许多具有弱注释数据的模式识别任务。人工神经网络和多实例学习的结合提供了端到端的解决方案并已得到广泛应用。然而，挑战仍然存在两个方面。首先，当前的 MIL 池化算子通常是预先定义的，缺乏挖掘关键实例的灵活性。其次，在当前的解决方案中，包级表示可能不准确或不可访问。为此，我们在本文中提出了一种注意意识多实例神经网络框架。它由实例级分类器、基于空间注意力的可训练 MIL 池化算子和包级分类层组成。对一系列模式识别任务的详尽实验表明，我们的框架优于许多最先进的 MIL 方法，并验证了我们提出的注意力 MIL 池化算子的有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种结合注意力机制的多实例神经网络框架，以解决多实例学习中关键实例挖掘不足和包级表示不准确的问题。
AI.motivation: 现有MIL方法的池化操作符缺乏灵活性，难以挖掘关键实例，且包级表示常存在不准确或不可访问的问题。
AI.method: 设计包含实例级分类器、基于空间注意力的可训练MIL池化操作符以及包级分类层的三阶段框架。
AI.result: 在多个模式识别任务中实验表明，该框架优于多种最新MIL方法，验证了注意力池化机制的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 提出的注意力感知框架通过动态实例筛选和精确包级建模，显著提升了多实例学习的性能。

authors_text: Min Li,Zhengyuan Shi,Qiuxia Lai,Sadaf Khan,Shaowei Cai,Qiang Xu
title: DeepSAT: An EDA-Driven Learning Framework for SAT
title_zh: DeepSAT：EDA 驱动的 SAT 学习框架
abstract: We present DeepSAT, a novel end-to-end learning framework for the Boolean satisfiability (SAT) problem. Unlike existing solutions trained on random SAT instances with relatively weak supervision, we propose applying the knowledge of the well-developed electronic design automation (EDA) field for SAT solving. Specifically, we first resort to logic synthesis algorithms to pre-process SAT instances into optimized and-inverter graphs (AIGs). By doing so, the distribution diversity among various SAT instances can be dramatically reduced, which facilitates improving the generalization capability of the learned model. Next, we regard the distribution of SAT solutions being a product of conditional Bernoulli distributions. Based on this observation, we approximate the SAT solving procedure with a conditional generative model, leveraging a novel directed acyclic graph neural network (DAGNN) with two polarity prototypes for conditional SAT modeling. To effectively train the generative model, with the help of logic simulation tools, we obtain the probabilities of nodes in the AIG being logic `1' as rich supervision. We conduct comprehensive experiments on various SAT problems. Our results show that, DeepSAT achieves significant accuracy improvements over state-of-the-art learning-based SAT solutions, especially when generalized to SAT instances that are relatively large or with diverse distributions.
summary: We present DeepSAT, a novel end-to-end learning framework for the Boolean satisfiability (SAT) problem. Unlike existing solutions trained on random SAT instances with relatively weak supervision, we propose applying the knowledge of the well-developed electronic design automation (EDA) field for SAT solving. Specifically, we first resort to logic synthesis algorithms to pre-process SAT instances into optimized and-inverter graphs (AIGs). By doing so, the distribution diversity among various SAT instances can be dramatically reduced, which facilitates improving the generalization capability of the learned model. Next, we regard the distribution of SAT solutions being a product of conditional Bernoulli distributions. Based on this observation, we approximate the SAT solving procedure with a conditional generative model, leveraging a novel directed acyclic graph neural network (DAGNN) with two polarity prototypes for conditional SAT modeling. To effectively train the generative model, with the help of logic simulation tools, we obtain the probabilities of nodes in the AIG being logic `1' as rich supervision. We conduct comprehensive experiments on various SAT problems. Our results show that, DeepSAT achieves significant accuracy improvements over state-of-the-art learning-based SAT solutions, especially when generalized to SAT instances that are relatively large or with diverse distributions.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了 DeepSAT，一种针对布尔可满足性（SAT）问题的新颖的端到端学习框架。与在监督相对较弱的随机 SAT 实例上训练的现有解决方案不同，我们建议应用成熟的电子设计自动化 (EDA) 领域的知识来解决 SAT 问题。具体来说，我们首先采用逻辑综合算法将 SAT 实例预处理为优化的逆变器图（AIG）。通过这样做，可以大大减少各种 SAT 实例之间的分布多样性，这有利于提高学习模型的泛化能力。接下来，我们将 SAT 解的分布视为条件伯努利分布的乘积。基于这一观察，我们使用条件生成模型来近似 SAT 求解过程，利用新颖的有向无环图神经网络 (DAGNN) 和两个极性原型进行条件 SAT 建模。为了有效地训练生成模型，借助逻辑仿真工具，我们获得AIG中节点为逻辑“1”的概率作为丰富的监督。我们对各种 SAT 问题进行了全面的实验。我们的结果表明，与最先进的基于学习的 SAT 解决方案相比，DeepSAT 的准确性显着提高，特别是在推广到相对较大或具有不同分布的 SAT 实例时。

AI.tldr: 本文提出DeepSAT，一种结合电子设计自动化（EDA）技术的端到端学习框架，用于解决布尔可满足性（SAT）问题。
AI.motivation: 现有SAT求解方法依赖随机实例和弱监督信号，难以提升模型泛化能力。通过引入EDA领域的知识，可优化问题表示并增强模型性能。
AI.method: 首先使用逻辑综合算法将SAT实例预处理为优化的与非图（AIG），降低分布多样性；其次将SAT解视为条件伯努利分布的乘积，设计基于有向无环图神经网络（DAGNN）的条件生成模型，并通过逻辑仿真工具获取节点逻辑'1'概率作为监督信号。
AI.result: 在多种SAT问题上的实验表明，DeepSAT在准确率上显著优于现有学习型SAT求解方法，尤其在处理大规模或分布多样化的实例时表现突出。
AI.conclusion: 将EDA技术与深度学习结合，能有效提升SAT求解的泛化能力和准确性，为相关领域提供新的研究方向。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出DeepSAT，一种结合电子设计自动化（EDA）技术的端到端学习框架，用于解决布尔可满足性（SAT）问题。
ai_content.motivation: 现有SAT求解方法依赖随机实例和弱监督信号，难以提升模型泛化能力。通过引入EDA领域的知识，可优化问题表示并增强模型性能。

search_text: DeepSAT: An EDA-Driven Learning Framework for SAT DeepSAT：EDA 驱动的 SAT 学习框架 We present DeepSAT, a novel end-to-end learning framework for the Boolean satisfiability (SAT) problem. Unlike existing solutions trained on random SAT instances with relatively weak supervision, we propose applying the knowledge of the well-developed electronic design automation (EDA) field for SAT solving. Specifically, we first resort to logic synthesis algorithms to pre-process SAT instances into optimized and-inverter graphs (AIGs). By doing so, the distribution diversity among various SAT instances can be dramatically reduced, which facilitates improving the generalization capability of the learned model. Next, we regard the distribution of SAT solutions being a product of conditional Bernoulli distributions. Based on this observation, we approximate the SAT solving procedure with a conditional generative model, leveraging a novel directed acyclic graph neural network (DAGNN) with two polarity prototypes for conditional SAT modeling. To effectively train the generative model, with the help of logic simulation tools, we obtain the probabilities of nodes in the AIG being logic `1' as rich supervision. We conduct comprehensive experiments on various SAT problems. Our results show that, DeepSAT achieves significant accuracy improvements over state-of-the-art learning-based SAT solutions, especially when generalized to SAT instances that are relatively large or with diverse distributions. 我们提出了 DeepSAT，一种针对布尔可满足性（SAT）问题的新颖的端到端学习框架。与在监督相对较弱的随机 SAT 实例上训练的现有解决方案不同，我们建议应用成熟的电子设计自动化 (EDA) 领域的知识来解决 SAT 问题。具体来说，我们首先采用逻辑综合算法将 SAT 实例预处理为优化的逆变器图（AIG）。通过这样做，可以大大减少各种 SAT 实例之间的分布多样性，这有利于提高学习模型的泛化能力。接下来，我们将 SAT 解的分布视为条件伯努利分布的乘积。基于这一观察，我们使用条件生成模型来近似 SAT 求解过程，利用新颖的有向无环图神经网络 (DAGNN) 和两个极性原型进行条件 SAT 建模。为了有效地训练生成模型，借助逻辑仿真工具，我们获得AIG中节点为逻辑“1”的概率作为丰富的监督。我们对各种 SAT 问题进行了全面的实验。我们的结果表明，与最先进的基于学习的 SAT 解决方案相比，DeepSAT 的准确性显着提高，特别是在推广到相对较大或具有不同分布的 SAT 实例时。 本文提出DeepSAT，一种结合电子设计自动化（EDA）技术的端到端学习框架，用于解决布尔可满足性（SAT）问题。 Min Li,Zhengyuan Shi,Qiuxia Lai,Sadaf Khan,Shaowei Cai,Qiang Xu cs.AI

authors_text: Jingjun Yi,Beichen Zhou
title: Learning Instance Representation Banks for Aerial Scene Classification
title_zh: 学习用于航空场景分类的实例表示库
abstract: Aerial scenes are more complicated in terms of object distribution and spatial arrangement than natural scenes due to the bird view, and thus remain challenging to learn discriminative scene representation. Recent solutions design \textit{local semantic descriptors} so that region of interests (RoIs) can be properly highlighted. However, each local descriptor has limited description capability and the overall scene representation remains to be refined. In this paper, we solve this problem by designing a novel representation set named \textit{instance representation bank} (IRB), which unifies multiple local descriptors under the multiple instance learning (MIL) formulation. This unified framework is not trivial as all the local semantic descriptors can be aligned to the same scene scheme, enhancing the scene representation capability. Specifically, our IRB learning framework consists of a backbone, an instance representation bank, a semantic fusion module and a scene scheme alignment loss function. All the components are organized in an end-to-end manner. Extensive experiments on three aerial scene benchmarks demonstrate that our proposed method outperforms the state-of-the-art approaches by a large margin.
summary: Aerial scenes are more complicated in terms of object distribution and spatial arrangement than natural scenes due to the bird view, and thus remain challenging to learn discriminative scene representation. Recent solutions design \textit{local semantic descriptors} so that region of interests (RoIs) can be properly highlighted. However, each local descriptor has limited description capability and the overall scene representation remains to be refined. In this paper, we solve this problem by designing a novel representation set named \textit{instance representation bank} (IRB), which unifies multiple local descriptors under the multiple instance learning (MIL) formulation. This unified framework is not trivial as all the local semantic descriptors can be aligned to the same scene scheme, enhancing the scene representation capability. Specifically, our IRB learning framework consists of a backbone, an instance representation bank, a semantic fusion module and a scene scheme alignment loss function. All the components are organized in an end-to-end manner. Extensive experiments on three aerial scene benchmarks demonstrate that our proposed method outperforms the state-of-the-art approaches by a large margin.
abstract_zh: 由于鸟瞰，空中场景在对象分布和空间排列方面比自然场景更加复杂，因此学习判别性场景表示仍然具有挑战性。最近的解决方案设计了\textit{局部语义描述符}，以便可以正确突出显示感兴趣区域（RoIs）。然而，每个局部描述符的描述能力有限，整体场景表示仍有待完善。在本文中，我们通过设计一个名为\textit{实例表示库}（IRB）的新颖表示集来解决这个问题，它统一了多实例学习（MIL）公式下的多个局部描述符。这个统一的框架并不简单，因为所有局部语义描述符都可以与相同的场景方案对齐，从而增强场景表示能力。具体来说，我们的 IRB 学习框架由主干、实例表示库、语义融合模块和场景方案对齐损失函数组成。所有组件都以端到端的方式组织。对三个航空场景基准的广泛实验表明，我们提出的方法大大优于最先进的方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种名为实例表示库（IRB）的新框架，用于解决航拍场景分类中局部描述符描述能力有限的问题。
AI.motivation: 航拍场景因物体分布和空间布局复杂，导致学习判别性表示困难。现有局部描述符能力有限，需统一和优化。
AI.method: 设计包含主干网络、实例表示库、语义融合模块和场景对齐损失函数的端到端框架，统一多局部描述符。
AI.result: 在三个航拍场景基准测试中，该方法显著优于现有最先进方法。
AI.conclusion: IRB通过统一局部描述符并增强场景表示能力，有效提升了航拍场景分类性能。

authors_text: Giovanni De Toni,Paolo Viappiani,Stefano Teso,Bruno Lepri,Andrea Passerini
title: Personalized Algorithmic Recourse with Preference Elicitation
title_zh: 具有偏好诱导的个性化算法资源
comment: Published in Transactions in Machine Learning Research (TMLR), January 2024. See https://openreview.net/forum?id=8sg2I9zXgO for the official submission
comments: Published in Transactions in Machine Learning Research (TMLR), January 2024. See https://openreview.net/forum?id=8sg2I9zXgO for the official submission
abstract: Algorithmic Recourse (AR) is the problem of computing a sequence of actions that -- once performed by a user -- overturns an undesirable machine decision. It is paramount that the sequence of actions does not require too much effort for users to implement. Yet, most approaches to AR assume that actions cost the same for all users, and thus may recommend unfairly expensive recourse plans to certain users. Prompted by this observation, we introduce PEAR, the first human-in-the-loop approach capable of providing personalized algorithmic recourse tailored to the needs of any end-user. PEAR builds on insights from Bayesian Preference Elicitation to iteratively refine an estimate of the costs of actions by asking choice set queries to the target user. The queries themselves are computed by maximizing the Expected Utility of Selection, a principled measure of information gain accounting for uncertainty on both the cost estimate and the user's responses. PEAR integrates elicitation into a Reinforcement Learning agent coupled with Monte Carlo Tree Search to quickly identify promising recourse plans. Our empirical evaluation on real-world datasets highlights how PEAR produces high-quality personalized recourse in only a handful of iterations.
summary: Algorithmic Recourse (AR) is the problem of computing a sequence of actions that -- once performed by a user -- overturns an undesirable machine decision. It is paramount that the sequence of actions does not require too much effort for users to implement. Yet, most approaches to AR assume that actions cost the same for all users, and thus may recommend unfairly expensive recourse plans to certain users. Prompted by this observation, we introduce PEAR, the first human-in-the-loop approach capable of providing personalized algorithmic recourse tailored to the needs of any end-user. PEAR builds on insights from Bayesian Preference Elicitation to iteratively refine an estimate of the costs of actions by asking choice set queries to the target user. The queries themselves are computed by maximizing the Expected Utility of Selection, a principled measure of information gain accounting for uncertainty on both the cost estimate and the user's responses. PEAR integrates elicitation into a Reinforcement Learning agent coupled with Monte Carlo Tree Search to quickly identify promising recourse plans. Our empirical evaluation on real-world datasets highlights how PEAR produces high-quality personalized recourse in only a handful of iterations.
abstract_zh: 算法资源（AR）是计算一系列动作的问题，这些动作一旦由用户执行，就会推翻不需要的机器决策。最重要的是，操作顺序不需要用户花费太多精力来实施。然而，大多数 AR 方法都假设所有用户的操作成本相同，因此可能会向某些用户推荐不公平昂贵的追索计划。受这一观察的启发，我们推出了 PEAR，这是第一个人机交互方法，能够根据任何最终用户的需求提供个性化的算法资源。 PEAR 基于贝叶斯偏好诱导的见解，通过向目标用户询问选择集查询来迭代地完善对操作成本的估计。查询本身是通过最大化选择的预期效用来计算的，这是一种信息增益的原则性衡量标准，考虑了成本估算和用户响应的不确定性。 PEAR 将启发集成到强化学习代理中，并结合蒙特卡罗树搜索来快速识别有前途的资源计划。我们对现实世界数据集的实证评估强调了 PEAR 如何在几次迭代中产生高质量的个性化资源。

AI.tldr: 本文提出PEAR方法，通过结合用户偏好获取与强化学习，实现个性化算法补救方案生成。
AI.motivation: 现有算法补救方法假设所有用户行动成本相同，可能导致对特定用户推荐高成本方案，缺乏公平性与个性化。
AI.method: 基于贝叶斯偏好获取，通过迭代选择集查询优化成本估计，并整合强化学习与蒙特卡洛树搜索快速生成补救计划。
AI.result: 在真实数据集上验证PEAR能在少量迭代中生成高质量的个性化补救方案，显著优于传统方法。
AI.conclusion: PEAR通过用户反馈与高效算法结合，有效解决了算法补救中的个性化与成本公平性问题。

The queries themselves are computed by maximizing the Expected Utility of Selection, a principled measure of information gain accounting for uncertainty on both the cost estimate and the user's responses. PEAR integrates elicitation into a Reinforcement Learning agent coupled with Monte Carlo Tree Search to quickly identify promising recourse plans. Our empirical evaluation on real-world datasets highlights how PEAR produces high-quality personalized recourse in only a handful of iterations. 算法资源（AR）是计算一系列动作的问题，这些动作一旦由用户执行，就会推翻不需要的机器决策。最重要的是，操作顺序不需要用户花费太多精力来实施。然而，大多数 AR 方法都假设所有用户的操作成本相同，因此可能会向某些用户推荐不公平昂贵的追索计划。受这一观察的启发，我们推出了 PEAR，这是第一个人机交互方法，能够根据任何最终用户的需求提供个性化的算法资源。 PEAR 基于贝叶斯偏好诱导的见解，通过向目标用户询问选择集查询来迭代地完善对操作成本的估计。查询本身是通过最大化选择的预期效用来计算的，这是一种信息增益的原则性衡量标准，考虑了成本估算和用户响应的不确定性。 PEAR 将启发集成到强化学习代理中，并结合蒙特卡罗树搜索来快速识别有前途的资源计划。我们对现实世界数据集的实证评估强调了 PEAR 如何在几次迭代中产生高质量的个性化资源。 本文提出PEAR方法，通过结合用户偏好获取与强化学习，实现个性化算法补救方案生成。 Giovanni De Toni,Paolo Viappiani,Stefano Teso,Bruno Lepri,Andrea Passerini cs.LG,cs.AI

authors_text: Christina Lu,Jackie Kay,Kevin R. McKee
title: Subverting machines, fluctuating identities: Re-learning human categorization
title_zh: 颠覆机器，波动身份：重新学习人类分类
comment: In Proceedings of the 2022 ACM Conference on Fairness, Accountability, and Transparency (FAccT '22), June 21-24, 2022, Seoul, Republic of Korea. First two authors contributed equally to this work
comments: In Proceedings of the 2022 ACM Conference on Fairness, Accountability, and Transparency (FAccT '22), June 21-24, 2022, Seoul, Republic of Korea. First two authors contributed equally to this work
abstract: Most machine learning systems that interact with humans construct some notion of a person's "identity," yet the default paradigm in AI research envisions identity with essential attributes that are discrete and static. In stark contrast, strands of thought within critical theory present a conception of identity as malleable and constructed entirely through interaction; a doing rather than a being. In this work, we distill some of these ideas for machine learning practitioners and introduce a theory of identity as autopoiesis, circular processes of formation and function. We argue that the default paradigm of identity used by the field immobilizes existing identity categories and the power differentials that co$\unicode{x2010}$occur, due to the absence of iterative feedback to our models. This includes a critique of emergent AI fairness practices that continue to impose the default paradigm. Finally, we apply our theory to sketch approaches to autopoietic identity through multilevel optimization and relational learning. While these ideas raise many open questions, we imagine the possibilities of machines that are capable of expressing human identity as a relationship perpetually in flux.
summary: Most machine learning systems that interact with humans construct some notion of a person's "identity," yet the default paradigm in AI research envisions identity with essential attributes that are discrete and static. In stark contrast, strands of thought within critical theory present a conception of identity as malleable and constructed entirely through interaction; a doing rather than a being. In this work, we distill some of these ideas for machine learning practitioners and introduce a theory of identity as autopoiesis, circular processes of formation and function. We argue that the default paradigm of identity used by the field immobilizes existing identity categories and the power differentials that co$\unicode{x2010}$occur, due to the absence of iterative feedback to our models. This includes a critique of emergent AI fairness practices that continue to impose the default paradigm. Finally, we apply our theory to sketch approaches to autopoietic identity through multilevel optimization and relational learning. While these ideas raise many open questions, we imagine the possibilities of machines that are capable of expressing human identity as a relationship perpetually in flux.
abstract_zh: 大多数与人类交互的机器学习系统都会构建一些人的“身份”概念，但人工智能研究中的默认范式设想身份具有离散和静态的基本属性。与此形成鲜明对比的是，批判理论中的思想流派提出了一种身份概念，即身份是可塑的，并且完全是通过互动来构建的。是一种行为，而不是一种存在。在这项工作中，我们为机器学习从业者提炼了其中一些想法，并引入了一种身份理论，即自创生、形成和功能的循环过程。我们认为，由于我们的模型缺乏迭代反馈，该领域使用的默认身份范式固定了现有的身份类别和 co$\unicode{x2010}$ 发生的权力差异。这包括对继续强加默认范式的新兴人工智能公平实践的批评。最后，我们应用我们的理论通过多级优化和关系学习来勾画自创生身份的方法。虽然这些想法提出了许多悬而未决的问题，但我们想象机器能够将人类身份表达为一种永远变化的关系的可能性。

AI.tldr: 本文挑战传统AI中静态身份观念，提出身份应视为动态交互过程，并引入自创生理论指导机器学习实践。
AI.motivation: 现有AI系统将身份视为离散静态属性，忽视了身份的社会建构性和流动性，导致模型固化权力差异并无法适应真实的人类身份复杂性。
AI.method: 提出身份自创生理论，通过多级优化和关系学习方法，构建动态反馈机制以模拟身份的持续生成过程。
AI.result: 建立理论框架指出传统身份模型的局限性，并为动态身份建模提供新思路，但相关技术实现仍需进一步探索。
AI.conclusion: 需重新思考身份的动态本质，通过自创生理论推动AI系统更公平地处理人类身份的复杂性与流动性。

authors_text: Yuxi Cai,Huicheng Lai
title: Image Reconstruction of Multi Branch Feature Multiplexing Fusion Network with Mixed Multi-layer Attention
title_zh: 混合多层注意力的多分支特征复用融合网络的图像重建
abstract: Image super-resolution reconstruction achieves better results than traditional methods with the help of the powerful nonlinear representation ability of convolution neural network. However, some existing algorithms also have some problems, such as insufficient utilization of phased features, ignoring the importance of early phased feature fusion to improve network performance, and the inability of the network to pay more attention to high-frequency information in the reconstruction process. To solve these problems, we propose a multi-branch feature multiplexing fusion network with mixed multi-layer attention (MBMFN), which realizes the multiple utilization of features and the multistage fusion of different levels of features. To further improve the networks performance, we propose a lightweight enhanced residual channel attention (LERCA), which can not only effectively avoid the loss of channel information but also make the network pay more attention to the key channel information and benefit from it. Finally, the attention mechanism is introduced into the reconstruction process to strengthen the restoration of edge texture and other details. A large number of experiments on several benchmark sets show that, compared with other advanced reconstruction algorithms, our algorithm produces highly competitive objective indicators and restores more image detail texture information.
summary: Image super-resolution reconstruction achieves better results than traditional methods with the help of the powerful nonlinear representation ability of convolution neural network. However, some existing algorithms also have some problems, such as insufficient utilization of phased features, ignoring the importance of early phased feature fusion to improve network performance, and the inability of the network to pay more attention to high-frequency information in the reconstruction process. To solve these problems, we propose a multi-branch feature multiplexing fusion network with mixed multi-layer attention (MBMFN), which realizes the multiple utilization of features and the multistage fusion of different levels of features. To further improve the networks performance, we propose a lightweight enhanced residual channel attention (LERCA), which can not only effectively avoid the loss of channel information but also make the network pay more attention to the key channel information and benefit from it. Finally, the attention mechanism is introduced into the reconstruction process to strengthen the restoration of edge texture and other details. A large number of experiments on several benchmark sets show that, compared with other advanced reconstruction algorithms, our algorithm produces highly competitive objective indicators and restores more image detail texture information.
abstract_zh: 图像超分辨率重建借助卷积神经网络强大的非线性表示能力，取得了比传统方法更好的效果。然而，现有的一些算法也存在一些问题，如阶段性特征利用不足、忽视了早期阶段性特征融合对提高网络性能的重要性、网络在重建过程中无法更多地关注高频信息等。为了解决这些问题，我们提出了一种混合多层注意力的多分支特征复用融合网络（MBMFN），实现了特征的多重利用和不同层次特征的多级融合。为了进一步提高网络性能，我们提出了轻量级增强残差通道注意力（LERCA），它不仅可以有效避免通道信息的丢失，而且可以使网络更加关注关键通道信息并从中受益。最后，在重建过程中引入注意力机制，加强边缘纹理等细节的恢复。在多个基准集上的大量实验表明，与其他先进的重建算法相比，我们的算法产生了极具竞争力的客观指标，并恢复了更多的图像细节纹理信息。

AI.tldr: 提出一种结合多分支特征复用与混合多层注意力机制的网络，以提升图像超分辨率重建效果
AI.motivation: 现有算法存在阶段性特征利用不足、早期特征融合重要性被忽视及高频信息关注不足的问题，需通过多分支特征融合与注意力机制优化网络性能
AI.method: 提出多分支特征复用融合网络（MBMFN），结合混合多层注意力机制与轻量级增强残差通道注意力（LERCA），强化特征多阶段融合与关键通道信息关注
AI.result: 在多个基准数据集上实验表明，算法在客观指标和图像细节纹理恢复上优于其他先进重建算法
AI.conclusion: 通过多分支特征融合与混合注意力机制，有效提升了图像超分辨率重建质量与细节保留能力

authors_text: Tingting Liu,Chengyu Wang,Cen Chen,Ming Gao,Aoying Zhou
title: Understanding Long Programming Languages with Structure-Aware Sparse Attention
title_zh: 通过结构感知的稀疏注意力来理解长编程语言
comment: sigir 2022 accepted, code will be available at https://github.com/alibaba/EasyNLP
comments: sigir 2022 accepted, code will be available at https://github.com/alibaba/EasyNLP
abstract: Programming-based Pre-trained Language Models (PPLMs) such as CodeBERT have achieved great success in many downstream code-related tasks. Since the memory and computational complexity of self-attention in the Transformer grow quadratically with the sequence length, PPLMs typically limit the code length to 512. However, codes in real-world applications are generally long, such as code searches, which cannot be processed efficiently by existing PPLMs. To solve this problem, in this paper, we present SASA, a Structure-Aware Sparse Attention mechanism, which reduces the complexity and improves performance for long code understanding tasks. The key components in SASA are top-$k$ sparse attention and Abstract Syntax Tree (AST)-based structure-aware attention. With top-$k$ sparse attention, the most crucial attention relation can be obtained with a lower computational cost. As the code structure represents the logic of the code statements, which is a complement to the code sequence characteristics, we further introduce AST structures into attention. Extensive experiments on CodeXGLUE tasks show that SASA achieves better performance than the competing baselines.
summary: Programming-based Pre-trained Language Models (PPLMs) such as CodeBERT have achieved great success in many downstream code-related tasks. Since the memory and computational complexity of self-attention in the Transformer grow quadratically with the sequence length, PPLMs typically limit the code length to 512. However, codes in real-world applications are generally long, such as code searches, which cannot be processed efficiently by existing PPLMs. To solve this problem, in this paper, we present SASA, a Structure-Aware Sparse Attention mechanism, which reduces the complexity and improves performance for long code understanding tasks. The key components in SASA are top-$k$ sparse attention and Abstract Syntax Tree (AST)-based structure-aware attention. With top-$k$ sparse attention, the most crucial attention relation can be obtained with a lower computational cost. As the code structure represents the logic of the code statements, which is a complement to the code sequence characteristics, we further introduce AST structures into attention. Extensive experiments on CodeXGLUE tasks show that SASA achieves better performance than the competing baselines.
abstract_zh: CodeBERT 等基于编程的预训练语言模型 (PPLM) 在许多下游代码相关任务中取得了巨大成功。由于 Transformer 中 self-attention 的内存和计算复杂度随序列长度呈二次方增长，因此 PPLM 通常将代码长度限制为 512。然而，现实应用中的代码通常很长，例如代码搜索，现有 PPLM 无法有效处理这些代码。为了解决这个问题，在本文中，我们提出了 SASA，一种结构感知的稀疏注意力机制，它降低了长代码理解任务的复杂性并提高了性能。 SASA 的关键组件是 top-$k$ 稀疏注意力和基于抽象语法树 (AST) 的结构感知注意力。通过top-$k$稀疏注意力，可以用较低的计算成本获得最关键的注意力关系。由于代码结构代表了代码语句的逻辑，是对代码序列特征的补充，因此我们进一步将 AST 结构引入注意力。对 CodeXGLUE 任务的大量实验表明，SASA 比竞争基线获得了更好的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种结构感知的稀疏注意力机制SASA，用于提升预训练语言模型在长代码理解任务中的性能。
AI.motivation: 现有基于Transformer的代码预训练模型（如CodeBERT）因自注意力机制的二次复杂度，通常限制代码长度为512，难以高效处理实际应用中常见的长代码（如代码搜索）。
AI.method: SASA包含两个核心组件：1）top-k稀疏注意力，通过选择关键注意力关系降低计算成本；2）基于抽象语法树（AST）的结构感知注意力，利用代码结构补充序列特征。
AI.result: 在CodeXGLUE任务上的实验表明，SASA在性能上优于现有基线方法。
AI.conclusion: SASA通过结合稀疏注意力与AST结构，有效解决了长代码处理中的计算复杂度与性能瓶颈问题。

search_text: Understanding Long Programming Languages with Structure-Aware Sparse Attention 通过结构感知的稀疏注意力来理解长编程语言 Programming-based Pre-trained Language Models (PPLMs) such as CodeBERT have achieved great success in many downstream code-related tasks. Since the memory and computational complexity of self-attention in the Transformer grow quadratically with the sequence length, PPLMs typically limit the code length to 512. However, codes in real-world applications are generally long, such as code searches, which cannot be processed efficiently by existing PPLMs. To solve this problem, in this paper, we present SASA, a Structure-Aware Sparse Attention mechanism, which reduces the complexity and improves performance for long code understanding tasks. The key components in SASA are top-$k$ sparse attention and Abstract Syntax Tree (AST)-based structure-aware attention. With top-$k$ sparse attention, the most crucial attention relation can be obtained with a lower computational cost. As the code structure represents the logic of the code statements, which is a complement to the code sequence characteristics, we further introduce AST structures into attention. Extensive experiments on CodeXGLUE tasks show that SASA achieves better performance than the competing baselines. CodeBERT 等基于编程的预训练语言模型 (PPLM) 在许多下游代码相关任务中取得了巨大成功。由于 Transformer 中 self-attention 的内存和计算复杂度随序列长度呈二次方增长，因此 PPLM 通常将代码长度限制为 512。然而，现实应用中的代码通常很长，例如代码搜索，现有 PPLM 无法有效处理这些代码。为了解决这个问题，在本文中，我们提出了 SASA，一种结构感知的稀疏注意力机制，它降低了长代码理解任务的复杂性并提高了性能。 SASA 的关键组件是 top-$k$ 稀疏注意力和基于抽象语法树 (AST) 的结构感知注意力。通过top-$k$稀疏注意力，可以用较低的计算成本获得最关键的注意力关系。由于代码结构代表了代码语句的逻辑，是对代码序列特征的补充，因此我们进一步将 AST 结构引入注意力。对 CodeXGLUE 任务的大量实验表明，SASA 比竞争基线获得了更好的性能。 本文提出了一种结构感知的稀疏注意力机制SASA，用于提升预训练语言模型在长代码理解任务中的性能。 Tingting Liu,Chengyu Wang,Cen Chen,Ming Gao,Aoying Zhou cs.CL,cs.AI

authors_text: Yushi Cao,Zhiming Li,Tianpei Yang,Hao Zhang,Yan Zheng,Yi Li,Jianye Hao,Yang Liu
title: GALOIS: Boosting Deep Reinforcement Learning via Generalizable Logic Synthesis
title_zh: GALOIS：通过可推广逻辑综合促进深度强化学习
abstract: Despite achieving superior performance in human-level control problems, unlike humans, deep reinforcement learning (DRL) lacks high-order intelligence (e.g., logic deduction and reuse), thus it behaves ineffectively than humans regarding learning and generalization in complex problems. Previous works attempt to directly synthesize a white-box logic program as the DRL policy, manifesting logic-driven behaviors. However, most synthesis methods are built on imperative or declarative programming, and each has a distinct limitation, respectively. The former ignores the cause-effect logic during synthesis, resulting in low generalizability across tasks. The latter is strictly proof-based, thus failing to synthesize programs with complex hierarchical logic. In this paper, we combine the above two paradigms together and propose a novel Generalizable Logic Synthesis (GALOIS) framework to synthesize hierarchical and strict cause-effect logic programs. GALOIS leverages the program sketch and defines a new sketch-based hybrid program language for guiding the synthesis. Based on that, GALOIS proposes a sketch-based program synthesis method to automatically generate white-box programs with generalizable and interpretable cause-effect logic. Extensive evaluations on various decision-making tasks with complex logic demonstrate the superiority of GALOIS over mainstream baselines regarding the asymptotic performance, generalizability, and great knowledge reusability across different environments.
summary: Despite achieving superior performance in human-level control problems, unlike humans, deep reinforcement learning (DRL) lacks high-order intelligence (e.g., logic deduction and reuse), thus it behaves ineffectively than humans regarding learning and generalization in complex problems. Previous works attempt to directly synthesize a white-box logic program as the DRL policy, manifesting logic-driven behaviors. However, most synthesis methods are built on imperative or declarative programming, and each has a distinct limitation, respectively. The former ignores the cause-effect logic during synthesis, resulting in low generalizability across tasks. The latter is strictly proof-based, thus failing to synthesize programs with complex hierarchical logic. In this paper, we combine the above two paradigms together and propose a novel Generalizable Logic Synthesis (GALOIS) framework to synthesize hierarchical and strict cause-effect logic programs. GALOIS leverages the program sketch and defines a new sketch-based hybrid program language for guiding the synthesis. Based on that, GALOIS proposes a sketch-based program synthesis method to automatically generate white-box programs with generalizable and interpretable cause-effect logic. Extensive evaluations on various decision-making tasks with complex logic demonstrate the superiority of GALOIS over mainstream baselines regarding the asymptotic performance, generalizability, and great knowledge reusability across different environments.
abstract_zh: 尽管深度强化学习（DRL）在人类水平的控制问题上取得了优异的表现，但与人类不同的是，深度强化学习（DRL）缺乏高阶智能（例如逻辑推导和重用），因此在复杂问题的学习和泛化方面表现不如人类。之前的工作尝试直接综合白盒逻辑程序作为DRL策略，体现逻辑驱动的行为。然而，大多数综合方法都是基于命令式或声明式编程构建的，并且每种方法分别具有明显的限制。前者在综合过程中忽略了因果逻辑，导致跨任务的通用性较低。后者是严格基于证明的，因此无法合成具有复杂层次逻辑的程序。在本文中，我们将上述两种范式结合在一起，提出了一种新颖的可广义逻辑综合（GALOIS）框架来综合分层且严格的因果逻辑程序。 GALOIS 利用程序草图并定义了一种新的基于草图的混合程序语言来指导综合。在此基础上，GALOIS提出了一种基于草图的程序综合方法，自动生成具有可泛化和可解释因果逻辑的白盒程序。对具有复杂逻辑的各种决策任务的广泛评估表明，GALOIS 在渐近性能、泛化性和跨不同环境的知识可重用性方面优于主流基线。

AI.tldr: 本文提出GALOIS框架，通过可泛化的逻辑合成提升深度强化学习的性能。
AI.motivation: 深度强化学习（DRL）缺乏逻辑推理和复用能力，导致在复杂任务中泛化能力不足。现有逻辑合成方法存在因果逻辑缺失或复杂逻辑难以合成的问题。
AI.method: 结合命令式与声明式编程范式，提出基于程序草图的混合语言和合成方法，生成具有可解释因果逻辑的白盒程序。
AI.result: 在多个复杂逻辑决策任务中，GALOIS在渐进性能、泛化能力和知识复用性上优于主流基线方法。
AI.conclusion: GALOIS通过整合两种编程范式，有效提升了DRL的逻辑推理能力与跨环境适应性。

authors_text: Yihao Ding,Zhe Huang,Runlin Wang,Yanhang Zhang,Xianru Chen,Yuzhong Ma,Hyunsuk Chung,Soyeon Caren Han
title: V-Doc : Visual questions answers with Documents
title_zh: V-Doc：用文档回答视觉问题
abstract: We propose V-Doc, a question-answering tool using document images and PDF, mainly for researchers and general non-deep learning experts looking to generate, process, and understand the document visual question answering tasks. The V-Doc supports generating and using both extractive and abstractive question-answer pairs using documents images. The extractive QA selects a subset of tokens or phrases from the document contents to predict the answers, while the abstractive QA recognises the language in the content and generates the answer based on the trained model. Both aspects are crucial to understanding the documents, especially in an image format. We include a detailed scenario of question generation for the abstractive QA task. V-Doc supports a wide range of datasets and models, and is highly extensible through a declarative, framework-agnostic platform.
summary: We propose V-Doc, a question-answering tool using document images and PDF, mainly for researchers and general non-deep learning experts looking to generate, process, and understand the document visual question answering tasks. The V-Doc supports generating and using both extractive and abstractive question-answer pairs using documents images. The extractive QA selects a subset of tokens or phrases from the document contents to predict the answers, while the abstractive QA recognises the language in the content and generates the answer based on the trained model. Both aspects are crucial to understanding the documents, especially in an image format. We include a detailed scenario of question generation for the abstractive QA task. V-Doc supports a wide range of datasets and models, and is highly extensible through a declarative, framework-agnostic platform.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了V-Doc，一种使用文档图像和PDF的问答工具，主要面向希望生成、处理和理解文档视觉问答任务的研究人员和一般非深度学习专家。 V-Doc 支持使用文档图像生成和使用提取和抽象问答对。提取式 QA 从文档内容中选择标记或短语的子集来预测答案，而抽象 QA 识别内容中的语言并根据训练的模型生成答案。这两个方面对于理解文档都至关重要，尤其是图像格式的文档。我们为抽象 QA 任务提供了问题生成的详细场景。 V-Doc 支持广泛的数据集和模型，并且可以通过声明性的、与框架无关的平台进行高度扩展。

AI.tldr: 提出V-Doc工具，利用文档图像和PDF生成抽取式和抽象式问答对，辅助理解和处理文档视觉问答任务。
AI.motivation: 现有工具难以满足研究人员和非深度学习专家对文档视觉问答任务的多样化需求，需更灵活且易用的解决方案。
AI.method: 结合抽取式（从文档中提取内容）与抽象式问答（基于训练模型生成答案）方法，采用声明式框架实现，支持多种数据集和模型。
AI.result: 验证了V-Doc在生成和处理文档视觉问答任务中的有效性，支持多模型多数据集，展示了其可扩展性。
AI.conclusion: V-Doc通过结合两种问答方法，为文档视觉问答任务提供了灵活且可扩展的解决方案，适用于多种场景和用户群体。

tldr: 提出V-Doc工具，利用文档图像和PDF生成抽取式和抽象式问答对，辅助理解和处理文档视觉问答任务。
motivation: 现有工具难以满足研究人员和非深度学习专家对文档视觉问答任务的多样化需求，需更灵活且易用的解决方案。
method: 结合抽取式（从文档中提取内容）与抽象式问答（基于训练模型生成答案）方法，采用声明式框架实现，支持多种数据集和模型。
result: 验证了V-Doc在生成和处理文档视觉问答任务中的有效性，支持多模型多数据集，展示了其可扩展性。
conclusion: V-Doc通过结合两种问答方法，为文档视觉问答任务提供了灵活且可扩展的解决方案，适用于多种场景和用户群体。
search_text: V-Doc : Visual questions answers with Documents V-Doc：用文档回答视觉问题 We propose V-Doc, a question-answering tool using document images and PDF, mainly for researchers and general non-deep learning experts looking to generate, process, and understand the document visual question answering tasks. The V-Doc supports generating and using both extractive and abstractive question-answer pairs using documents images. The extractive QA selects a subset of tokens or phrases from the document contents to predict the answers, while the abstractive QA recognises the language in the content and generates the answer based on the trained model. Both aspects are crucial to understanding the documents, especially in an image format. We include a detailed scenario of question generation for the abstractive QA task. V-Doc supports a wide range of datasets and models, and is highly extensible through a declarative, framework-agnostic platform. 我们提出了V-Doc，一种使用文档图像和PDF的问答工具，主要面向希望生成、处理和理解文档视觉问答任务的研究人员和一般非深度学习专家。 V-Doc 支持使用文档图像生成和使用提取和抽象问答对。提取式 QA 从文档内容中选择标记或短语的子集来预测答案，而抽象 QA 识别内容中的语言并根据训练的模型生成答案。这两个方面对于理解文档都至关重要，尤其是图像格式的文档。我们为抽象 QA 任务提供了问题生成的详细场景。 V-Doc 支持广泛的数据集和模型，并且可以通过声明性的、与框架无关的平台进行高度扩展。 提出V-Doc工具，利用文档图像和PDF生成抽取式和抽象式问答对，辅助理解和处理文档视觉问答任务。 Yihao Ding,Zhe Huang,Runlin Wang,Yanhang Zhang,Xianru Chen,Yuzhong Ma,Hyunsuk Chung,Soyeon Caren Han cs.AI,cs.CV

authors_text: Hongzheng Yang,Cheng Chen,Meirui Jiang,Quande Liu,Jianfeng Cao,Pheng Ann Heng,Qi Dou
title: DLTTA: Dynamic Learning Rate for Test-time Adaptation on Cross-domain Medical Images
title_zh: DLTTA：跨域医学图像测试时间适应的动态学习率
abstract: Test-time adaptation (TTA) has increasingly been an important topic to efficiently tackle the cross-domain distribution shift at test time for medical images from different institutions. Previous TTA methods have a common limitation of using a fixed learning rate for all the test samples. Such a practice would be sub-optimal for TTA, because test data may arrive sequentially therefore the scale of distribution shift would change frequently. To address this problem, we propose a novel dynamic learning rate adjustment method for test-time adaptation, called DLTTA, which dynamically modulates the amount of weights update for each test image to account for the differences in their distribution shift. Specifically, our DLTTA is equipped with a memory bank based estimation scheme to effectively measure the discrepancy of a given test sample. Based on this estimated discrepancy, a dynamic learning rate adjustment strategy is then developed to achieve a suitable degree of adaptation for each test sample. The effectiveness and general applicability of our DLTTA is extensively demonstrated on three tasks including retinal optical coherence tomography (OCT) segmentation, histopathological image classification, and prostate 3D MRI segmentation. Our method achieves effective and fast test-time adaptation with consistent performance improvement over current state-of-the-art test-time adaptation methods. Code is available at: https://github.com/med-air/DLTTA.
summary: Test-time adaptation (TTA) has increasingly been an important topic to efficiently tackle the cross-domain distribution shift at test time for medical images from different institutions. Previous TTA methods have a common limitation of using a fixed learning rate for all the test samples. Such a practice would be sub-optimal for TTA, because test data may arrive sequentially therefore the scale of distribution shift would change frequently. To address this problem, we propose a novel dynamic learning rate adjustment method for test-time adaptation, called DLTTA, which dynamically modulates the amount of weights update for each test image to account for the differences in their distribution shift. Specifically, our DLTTA is equipped with a memory bank based estimation scheme to effectively measure the discrepancy of a given test sample. Based on this estimated discrepancy, a dynamic learning rate adjustment strategy is then developed to achieve a suitable degree of adaptation for each test sample. The effectiveness and general applicability of our DLTTA is extensively demonstrated on three tasks including retinal optical coherence tomography (OCT) segmentation, histopathological image classification, and prostate 3D MRI segmentation. Our method achieves effective and fast test-time adaptation with consistent performance improvement over current state-of-the-art test-time adaptation methods. Code is available at: https://github.com/med-air/DLTTA.
abstract_zh: 测试时适应（TTA）已日益成为有效解决来自不同机构的医学图像在测试时的跨域分布变化的重要课题。以前的 TTA 方法有一个共同的限制，即对所有测试样本使用固定的学习率。这种做法对于 TTA 来说不是最佳选择，因为测试数据可能会按顺序到达，因此分布偏移的规模会频繁变化。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种用于测试时间适应的新型动态学习率调整方法，称为 DLTTA，它动态调整每个测试图像的权重更新量，以解决其分布变化的差异。具体来说，我们的 DLTTA 配备了基于内存库的估计方案，可以有效测量给定测试样本的差异。基于这种估计的差异，然后开发动态学习率调整策略，以实现每个测试样本的合适程度的适应。我们的 DLTTA 的有效性和普遍适用性在三个任务中得到了广泛的证明，包括视网膜光学相干断层扫描 (OCT) 分割、组织病理学图像分类和前列腺 3D MRI 分割。我们的方法实现了有效且快速的测试时间适应，并且比当前最先进的测试时间适应方法具有一致的性能改进。代码位于：https://github.com/med-air/DLTTA。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种动态学习率调整方法DLTTA，用于跨域医学图像的测试时适应（TTA），以应对测试数据分布变化带来的挑战。
AI.motivation: 现有TTA方法采用固定学习率处理所有测试样本，无法适应测试数据分布变化频繁的特点，导致适应效果次优。
AI.method: DLTTA通过记忆库估计测试样本的分布差异，并基于差异程度动态调整学习率，实现对每个样本的个性化权重更新。
AI.result: 在视网膜OCT分割、组织病理学图像分类和前列腺MRI分割三个任务中，DLTTA均表现出优于现有最先进方法的性能提升。
AI.conclusion: DLTTA通过动态学习率机制有效提升了跨域医学图像的测试时适应能力，具有广泛适用性和实际应用价值。

Previous TTA methods have a common limitation of using a fixed learning rate for all the test samples. Such a practice would be sub-optimal for TTA, because test data may arrive sequentially therefore the scale of distribution shift would change frequently. To address this problem, we propose a novel dynamic learning rate adjustment method for test-time adaptation, called DLTTA, which dynamically modulates the amount of weights update for each test image to account for the differences in their distribution shift. Specifically, our DLTTA is equipped with a memory bank based estimation scheme to effectively measure the discrepancy of a given test sample. Based on this estimated discrepancy, a dynamic learning rate adjustment strategy is then developed to achieve a suitable degree of adaptation for each test sample. The effectiveness and general applicability of our DLTTA is extensively demonstrated on three tasks including retinal optical coherence tomography (OCT) segmentation, histopathological image classification, and prostate 3D MRI segmentation. Our method achieves effective and fast test-time adaptation with consistent performance improvement over current state-of-the-art test-time adaptation methods. authors_text: Tao Zhuo,Mohan Kankanhalli
title: Effective Abstract Reasoning with Dual-Contrast Network
title_zh: 双对比网络的有效抽象推理
abstract: As a step towards improving the abstract reasoning capability of machines, we aim to solve Raven's Progressive Matrices (RPM) with neural networks, since solving RPM puzzles is highly correlated with human intelligence. Unlike previous methods that use auxiliary annotations or assume hidden rules to produce appropriate feature representation, we only use the ground truth answer of each question for model learning, aiming for an intelligent agent to have a strong learning capability with a small amount of supervision. Based on the RPM problem formulation, the correct answer filled into the missing entry of the third row/column has to best satisfy the same rules shared between the first two rows/columns. Thus we design a simple yet effective Dual-Contrast Network (DCNet) to exploit the inherent structure of RPM puzzles. Specifically, a rule contrast module is designed to compare the latent rules between the filled row/column and the first two rows/columns; a choice contrast module is designed to increase the relative differences between candidate choices. Experimental results on the RAVEN and PGM datasets show that DCNet outperforms the state-of-the-art methods by a large margin of 5.77%. Further experiments on few training samples and model generalization also show the effectiveness of DCNet. Code is available at https://github.com/visiontao/dcnet.
summary: As a step towards improving the abstract reasoning capability of machines, we aim to solve Raven's Progressive Matrices (RPM) with neural networks, since solving RPM puzzles is highly correlated with human intelligence. Unlike previous methods that use auxiliary annotations or assume hidden rules to produce appropriate feature representation, we only use the ground truth answer of each question for model learning, aiming for an intelligent agent to have a strong learning capability with a small amount of supervision. Based on the RPM problem formulation, the correct answer filled into the missing entry of the third row/column has to best satisfy the same rules shared between the first two rows/columns. Thus we design a simple yet effective Dual-Contrast Network (DCNet) to exploit the inherent structure of RPM puzzles. Specifically, a rule contrast module is designed to compare the latent rules between the filled row/column and the first two rows/columns; a choice contrast module is designed to increase the relative differences between candidate choices. Experimental results on the RAVEN and PGM datasets show that DCNet outperforms the state-of-the-art methods by a large margin of 5.77%. Further experiments on few training samples and model generalization also show the effectiveness of DCNet. Code is available at https://github.com/visiontao/dcnet.
abstract_zh: 作为提高机器抽象推理能力的一步，我们的目标是用神经网络解决瑞文渐进矩阵（RPM），因为解决 RPM 难题与人类智力高度相关。与以前使用辅助注释或假设隐藏规则来产生适当特征表示的方法不同，我们仅使用每个问题的真实答案进行模型学习，旨在使智能代理在少量监督下具有强大的学习能力。根据 RPM 问题公式，填充到第三行/列的缺失条目中的正确答案必须最好地满足前两行/列之间共享的相同规则。因此，我们设计了一个简单而有效的双对比网络（DCNet）来利用 RPM 谜题的固有结构。具体来说，设计了规则对比模块来比较填充的行/列与前两行/列之间的潜在规则；选择对比模块旨在增加候选选择之间的相对差异。 RAVEN 和 PGM 数据集上的实验结果表明，DCNet 的性能比最先进的方法高出 5.77%。进一步对少量训练样本和模型泛化的实验也显示了 DCNet 的有效性。代码可在 https://github.com/visiontao/dcnet 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为Dual-Contrast Network (DCNet)的神经网络模型，用于解决Raven's Progressive Matrices (RPM)题目，以提升机器的抽象推理能力。
AI.motivation: 传统方法依赖辅助标注或假设隐藏规则来生成特征表示，而本文旨在通过仅使用题目正确答案进行监督，使智能体在少量标注下具备强学习能力。
AI.method: 设计了包含规则对比模块和选项对比模块的DCNet。规则对比模块比较第三行/列与前两行/列的潜在规则，选项对比模块增强候选答案的相对差异。
AI.result: 在RAVEN和PGM数据集上，DCNet较现有方法提升5.77%，且在少量样本和模型泛化实验中表现优异。
AI.conclusion: DCNet通过挖掘RPM内在结构，实现了无需额外标注的高效抽象推理，验证了其在小样本和泛化场景下的有效性。

Experimental results on the RAVEN and PGM datasets show that DCNet outperforms the state-of-the-art methods by a large margin of 5.77%. Further experiments on few training samples and model generalization also show the effectiveness of DCNet. Code is available at https://github.com/visiontao/dcnet. 作为提高机器抽象推理能力的一步，我们的目标是用神经网络解决瑞文渐进矩阵（RPM），因为解决 RPM 难题与人类智力高度相关。与以前使用辅助注释或假设隐藏规则来产生适当特征表示的方法不同，我们仅使用每个问题的真实答案进行模型学习，旨在使智能代理在少量监督下具有强大的学习能力。根据 RPM 问题公式，填充到第三行/列的缺失条目中的正确答案必须最好地满足前两行/列之间共享的相同规则。因此，我们设计了一个简单而有效的双对比网络（DCNet）来利用 RPM 谜题的固有结构。具体来说，设计了规则对比模块来比较填充的行/列与前两行/列之间的潜在规则；选择对比模块旨在增加候选选择之间的相对差异。 RAVEN 和 PGM 数据集上的实验结果表明，DCNet 的性能比最先进的方法高出 5.77%。进一步对少量训练样本和模型泛化的实验也显示了 DCNet 的有效性。代码可在 https://github.com/visiontao/dcnet 获取。 本文提出了一种名为Dual-Contrast Network (DCNet)的神经网络模型，用于解决Raven's Progressive Matrices (RPM)题目，以提升机器的抽象推理能力。 Tao Zhuo,Mohan Kankanhalli cs.CV,cs.AI,cs.LG

authors_text: Wei Qiu,Weixun Wang,Rundong Wang,Bo An,Yujing Hu,Svetlana Obraztsova,Zinovi Rabinovich,Jianye Hao,Yingfeng Chen,Changjie Fan
title: Off-Beat Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
title_zh: 与众不同的多智能体强化学习
abstract: We investigate model-free multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) in environments where off-beat actions are prevalent, i.e., all actions have pre-set execution durations. During execution durations, the environment changes are influenced by, but not synchronised with, action execution. Such a setting is ubiquitous in many real-world problems. However, most MARL methods assume actions are executed immediately after inference, which is often unrealistic and can lead to catastrophic failure for multi-agent coordination with off-beat actions. In order to fill this gap, we develop an algorithmic framework for MARL with off-beat actions. We then propose a novel episodic memory, LeGEM, for model-free MARL algorithms. LeGEM builds agents' episodic memories by utilizing agents' individual experiences. It boosts multi-agent learning by addressing the challenging temporal credit assignment problem raised by the off-beat actions via our novel reward redistribution scheme, alleviating the issue of non-Markovian reward. We evaluate LeGEM on various multi-agent scenarios with off-beat actions, including Stag-Hunter Game, Quarry Game, Afforestation Game, and StarCraft II micromanagement tasks. Empirical results show that LeGEM significantly boosts multi-agent coordination and achieves leading performance and improved sample efficiency.
summary: We investigate model-free multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) in environments where off-beat actions are prevalent, i.e., all actions have pre-set execution durations. During execution durations, the environment changes are influenced by, but not synchronised with, action execution. Such a setting is ubiquitous in many real-world problems. However, most MARL methods assume actions are executed immediately after inference, which is often unrealistic and can lead to catastrophic failure for multi-agent coordination with off-beat actions. In order to fill this gap, we develop an algorithmic framework for MARL with off-beat actions. We then propose a novel episodic memory, LeGEM, for model-free MARL algorithms. LeGEM builds agents' episodic memories by utilizing agents' individual experiences. It boosts multi-agent learning by addressing the challenging temporal credit assignment problem raised by the off-beat actions via our novel reward redistribution scheme, alleviating the issue of non-Markovian reward. We evaluate LeGEM on various multi-agent scenarios with off-beat actions, including Stag-Hunter Game, Quarry Game, Afforestation Game, and StarCraft II micromanagement tasks. Empirical results show that LeGEM significantly boosts multi-agent coordination and achieves leading performance and improved sample efficiency.
abstract_zh: 我们在异常行为盛行的环境中研究无模型多智能体强化学习（MARL），即所有行为都有预设的执行持续时间。在执行期间，环境变化会受到动作执行的影响，但与动作执行不同步。这种设置在许多现实问题中普遍存在。然而，大多数 MARL 方法假设动作在推理后立即执行，这通常是不现实的，并且可能会导致多智能体协调与异常动作的灾难性失败。为了填补这一空白，我们开发了一个具有另类动作的 MARL 算法框架。然后，我们提出了一种新颖的情景记忆 LeGEM，用于无模型 MARL 算法。 LeGEM 通过利用代理人的个人经验来建立代理人的情景记忆。它通过我们新颖的奖励重新分配方案解决由非常规行为引起的具有挑战性的时间信用分配问题，从而促进多智能体学习，从而缓解非马尔可夫奖励问题。我们在各种具有另类动作的多智能体场景上评估 LeGEM，包括猎鹿游戏、采石场游戏、造林游戏和星际争霸 II 微管理任务。实证结果表明，LeGEM 显着促进了多智能体协调，实现了领先的性能并提高了样本效率。

AI.tldr: 本文研究了在存在离散动作（动作有预设执行时间）的环境中，如何改进多智能体强化学习（MARL）的算法框架。
AI.motivation: 现有MARL方法假设动作执行即时生效，但现实中动作执行需时间且环境变化与动作不同步，导致多智能体协调失败。
AI.method: 提出LeGEM算法，通过个体经验构建情节记忆，并设计奖励再分配方案解决非马尔可夫奖励问题。
AI.result: 在Stag-Hunter Game、StarCraft II等场景中，LeGEM显著提升多智能体协作效率与样本利用率。
AI.conclusion: LeGEM有效解决了离散动作下多智能体学习的时序信用分配难题，为现实场景提供可行方案。

During execution durations, the environment changes are influenced by, but not synchronised with, action execution. Such a setting is ubiquitous in many real-world problems. However, most MARL methods assume actions are executed immediately after inference, which is often unrealistic and can lead to catastrophic failure for multi-agent coordination with off-beat actions. In order to fill this gap, we develop an algorithmic framework for MARL with off-beat actions. We then propose a novel episodic memory, LeGEM, for model-free MARL algorithms. LeGEM builds agents' episodic memories by utilizing agents' individual experiences. It boosts multi-agent learning by addressing the challenging temporal credit assignment problem raised by the off-beat actions via our novel reward redistribution scheme, alleviating the issue of non-Markovian reward. We evaluate LeGEM on various multi-agent scenarios with off-beat actions, including Stag-Hunter Game, Quarry Game, Afforestation Game, and StarCraft II micromanagement tasks. authors_text: Hehe Fan,Xin Yu,Yuhang Ding,Yi Yang,Mohan Kankanhalli
title: PSTNet: Point Spatio-Temporal Convolution on Point Cloud Sequences
title_zh: PSTNet：点云序列上的点时空卷积
abstract: Point cloud sequences are irregular and unordered in the spatial dimension while exhibiting regularities and order in the temporal dimension. Therefore, existing grid based convolutions for conventional video processing cannot be directly applied to spatio-temporal modeling of raw point cloud sequences. In this paper, we propose a point spatio-temporal (PST) convolution to achieve informative representations of point cloud sequences. The proposed PST convolution first disentangles space and time in point cloud sequences. Then, a spatial convolution is employed to capture the local structure of points in the 3D space, and a temporal convolution is used to model the dynamics of the spatial regions along the time dimension. Furthermore, we incorporate the proposed PST convolution into a deep network, namely PSTNet, to extract features of point cloud sequences in a hierarchical manner. Extensive experiments on widely-used 3D action recognition and 4D semantic segmentation datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of PSTNet to model point cloud sequences.
summary: Point cloud sequences are irregular and unordered in the spatial dimension while exhibiting regularities and order in the temporal dimension. Therefore, existing grid based convolutions for conventional video processing cannot be directly applied to spatio-temporal modeling of raw point cloud sequences. In this paper, we propose a point spatio-temporal (PST) convolution to achieve informative representations of point cloud sequences. The proposed PST convolution first disentangles space and time in point cloud sequences. Then, a spatial convolution is employed to capture the local structure of points in the 3D space, and a temporal convolution is used to model the dynamics of the spatial regions along the time dimension. Furthermore, we incorporate the proposed PST convolution into a deep network, namely PSTNet, to extract features of point cloud sequences in a hierarchical manner. Extensive experiments on widely-used 3D action recognition and 4D semantic segmentation datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of PSTNet to model point cloud sequences.
abstract_zh: 点云序列在空间维度上是不规则和无序的，而在时间维度上表现出规律性和有序性。因此，现有的用于传统视频处理的基于网格的卷积不能直接应用于原始点云序列的时空建模。在本文中，我们提出了点时空（PST）卷积来实现点云序列的信息表示。所提出的 PST 卷积首先解开点云序列中的空间和时间。然后，使用空间卷积来捕获 3D 空间中点的局部结构，并使用时间卷积来模拟空间区域沿时间维度的动态。此外，我们将所提出的 PST 卷积合并到深度网络（即 PSTNet）中，以分层方式提取点云序列的特征。对广泛使用的 3D 动作识别和 4D 语义分割数据集进行的大量实验证明了 PSTNet 对点云序列建模的有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出PSTNet，通过点时空卷积对点云序列进行建模，以解决其在空间维度上的不规则性和时间维度上的规律性问题。
AI.motivation: 传统基于网格的卷积无法直接处理点云序列的空间不规则性，而现有方法未能有效结合点云的时空特性。
AI.method: 设计点时空卷积（PST卷积），分离空间与时间维度，利用空间卷积提取3D局部结构，时间卷积建模动态变化，并将其集成至深度网络PSTNet中进行层次化特征提取。
AI.result: 在常用3D动作识别和4D语义分割数据集上验证了PSTNet对点云序列建模的有效性。
AI.conclusion: PSTNet通过结合空间与时间卷积，有效捕捉点云序列的时空特征，在多个任务中表现出优越性能。

Therefore, existing grid based convolutions for conventional video processing cannot be directly applied to spatio-temporal modeling of raw point cloud sequences. The proposed PST convolution first disentangles space and time in point cloud sequences. Furthermore, we incorporate the proposed PST convolution into a deep network, namely PSTNet, to extract features of point cloud sequences in a hierarchical manner. authors_text: Shucheng Yang,Xiaoping Gao,Ruoting Yang,Jie Ren,Zhen Wang
title: A Hybrid Josephson Transmission Line and Passive Transmission Line Routing Framework for Single Flux Quantum Logic
title_zh: 用于单通量量子逻辑的混合约瑟夫森传输线和无源传输线路由框架
abstract: The Single Flux Quantum (SFQ) logic family is a novel digital logic as it provides ultra-fast and energy-efficient circuits. For large-scale SFQ circuit design, specialized electronic design automation (EDA) tools are required due to the differences in logic type, timing constraints and circuit architecture, in contrast to the CMOS logic. In order to improve the overall performance of an SFQ circuit, an efficient routing algorithm should be applied during the layout design to perform accurate timing adjustment for fixing hold violations and optimizing critical paths. Thus, a hybrid Josephson transmission line and passive transmission line routing framework is proposed. It consists of four main modules and an exploration of the potential timing performance based on the given layout placement. The proposed routing tool is demonstrated on seven testbench circuits. The obtained results demonstrate that the operating frequency is greatly improved, and all the hold violations are eliminated for each circuit.
summary: The Single Flux Quantum (SFQ) logic family is a novel digital logic as it provides ultra-fast and energy-efficient circuits. For large-scale SFQ circuit design, specialized electronic design automation (EDA) tools are required due to the differences in logic type, timing constraints and circuit architecture, in contrast to the CMOS logic. In order to improve the overall performance of an SFQ circuit, an efficient routing algorithm should be applied during the layout design to perform accurate timing adjustment for fixing hold violations and optimizing critical paths. Thus, a hybrid Josephson transmission line and passive transmission line routing framework is proposed. It consists of four main modules and an exploration of the potential timing performance based on the given layout placement. The proposed routing tool is demonstrated on seven testbench circuits. The obtained results demonstrate that the operating frequency is greatly improved, and all the hold violations are eliminated for each circuit.
abstract_zh: 单通量量子 (SFQ) 逻辑系列是一种新颖的数字逻辑，因为它提供超快速且节能的电路。对于大规模 SFQ 电路设计，由于逻辑类型、时序约束和电路架构与 CMOS 逻辑不同，因此需要专门的电子设计自动化 (EDA) 工具。为了提高SFQ电路的整体性能，在布局设计期间应采用高效的布线算法来执行精确的时序调整，以修复保持违规并优化关键路径。因此，提出了混合约瑟夫森传输线和无源传输线路由框架。它由四个主要模块组成，并根据给定的布局布局探索潜在的时序性能。所提出的布线工具在七个测试台电路上进行了演示。获得的结果表明，工作频率大大提高，并且每个电路的所有保持违规都被消除。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种结合约瑟夫森传输线和无源传输线的混合路由框架，用于优化单通量量子（SFQ）逻辑电路的布局设计。
AI.motivation: SFQ逻辑电路需要专门的电子设计自动化工具，以解决时序约束、保持违规和关键路径优化问题，从而提升大规模电路性能。
AI.method: 设计包含四个核心模块的混合路由框架，结合约瑟夫森传输线与无源传输线特性，并基于给定布局进行时序性能探索。

search_text: A Hybrid Josephson Transmission Line and Passive Transmission Line Routing Framework for Single Flux Quantum Logic 用于单通量量子逻辑的混合约瑟夫森传输线和无源传输线路由框架 The Single Flux Quantum (SFQ) logic family is a novel digital logic as it provides ultra-fast and energy-efficient circuits. For large-scale SFQ circuit design, specialized electronic design automation (EDA) tools are required due to the differences in logic type, timing constraints and circuit architecture, in contrast to the CMOS logic. In order to improve the overall performance of an SFQ circuit, an efficient routing algorithm should be applied during the layout design to perform accurate timing adjustment for fixing hold violations and optimizing critical paths. Thus, a hybrid Josephson transmission line and passive transmission line routing framework is proposed. It consists of four main modules and an exploration of the potential timing performance based on the given layout placement. The proposed routing tool is demonstrated on seven testbench circuits. The obtained results demonstrate that the operating frequency is greatly improved, and all the hold violations are eliminated for each circuit. 单通量量子 (SFQ) 逻辑系列是一种新颖的数字逻辑，因为它提供超快速且节能的电路。对于大规模 SFQ 电路设计，由于逻辑类型、时序约束和电路架构与 CMOS 逻辑不同，因此需要专门的电子设计自动化 (EDA) 工具。为了提高SFQ电路的整体性能，在布局设计期间应采用高效的布线算法来执行精确的时序调整，以修复保持违规并优化关键路径。因此，提出了混合约瑟夫森传输线和无源传输线路由框架。它由四个主要模块组成，并根据给定的布局布局探索潜在的时序性能。所提出的布线工具在七个测试台电路上进行了演示。获得的结果表明，工作频率大大提高，并且每个电路的所有保持违规都被消除。 本文提出了一种结合约瑟夫森传输线和无源传输线的混合路由框架，用于优化单通量量子（SFQ）逻辑电路的布局设计。 Shucheng Yang,Xiaoping Gao,Ruoting Yang,Jie Ren,Zhen Wang cs.ET,cs.AR

authors_text: Liam Hebert,Lukasz Golab,Pascal Poupart,Robin Cohen
title: FedFormer: Contextual Federation with Attention in Reinforcement Learning
title_zh: FedFormer：强化学习中关注的上下文联合
comment: Our source code can be found at https://github.com/liamhebert/FedFormer. Accepted at AAMAS 2023
comments: Our source code can be found at https://github.com/liamhebert/FedFormer. Accepted at AAMAS 2023
abstract: A core issue in multi-agent federated reinforcement learning is defining how to aggregate insights from multiple agents. This is commonly done by taking the average of each participating agent's model weights into one common model (FedAvg). We instead propose FedFormer, a novel federation strategy that utilizes Transformer Attention to contextually aggregate embeddings from models originating from different learner agents. In so doing, we attentively weigh the contributions of other agents with respect to the current agent's environment and learned relationships, thus providing a more effective and efficient federation. We evaluate our methods on the Meta-World environment and find that our approach yields significant improvements over FedAvg and non-federated Soft Actor-Critic single-agent methods. Our results compared to Soft Actor-Critic show that FedFormer achieves higher episodic return while still abiding by the privacy constraints of federated learning. Finally, we also demonstrate improvements in effectiveness with increased agent pools across all methods in certain tasks. This is contrasted by FedAvg, which fails to make noticeable improvements when scaled.
summary: A core issue in multi-agent federated reinforcement learning is defining how to aggregate insights from multiple agents. This is commonly done by taking the average of each participating agent's model weights into one common model (FedAvg). We instead propose FedFormer, a novel federation strategy that utilizes Transformer Attention to contextually aggregate embeddings from models originating from different learner agents. In so doing, we attentively weigh the contributions of other agents with respect to the current agent's environment and learned relationships, thus providing a more effective and efficient federation. We evaluate our methods on the Meta-World environment and find that our approach yields significant improvements over FedAvg and non-federated Soft Actor-Critic single-agent methods. Our results compared to Soft Actor-Critic show that FedFormer achieves higher episodic return while still abiding by the privacy constraints of federated learning. Finally, we also demonstrate improvements in effectiveness with increased agent pools across all methods in certain tasks. This is contrasted by FedAvg, which fails to make noticeable improvements when scaled.
abstract_zh: 多智能体联合强化学习的一个核心问题是定义如何聚合来自多个智能体的见解。这通常是通过将每个参与代理的模型权重的平均值代入一个公共模型 (FedAvg) 来完成的。相反，我们提出了 FedFormer，这是一种新颖的联合策略，它利用 Transformer Attention 根据上下文聚合来自不同学习代理的模型的嵌入。在此过程中，我们仔细权衡其他智能体对当前智能体环境和学习关系的贡献，从而提供更有效和高效的联合。我们在元世界环境中评估我们的方法，发现我们的方法比 FedAvg 和非联合 Soft Actor-Critic 单代理方法有了显着改进。与 Soft Actor-Critic 相比，我们的结果表明 FedFormer 实现了更高的情景回报，同时仍然遵守联邦学习的隐私限制。最后，我们还展示了在某些任务中通过增加所有方法的代理池来提高效率。这与 FedAvg 形成鲜明对比，FedAvg 在扩展时未能做出明显的改进。

AI.tldr: 本文提出FedFormer方法，利用Transformer注意力机制改进多智能体联邦强化学习中的模型聚合策略。
AI.motivation: 传统FedAvg方法简单平均模型权重，无法考虑不同智能体间的环境差异和关联性，导致聚合效率低下。
AI.method: 采用Transformer注意力机制，根据当前智能体的环境状态和与其他智能体的关联关系，动态调整模型聚合时的权重分配。
AI.result: 在Meta-World环境中，FedFormer相比FedAvg和非联邦方法取得更高回合回报，且在增加智能体数量时效果持续提升。
AI.conclusion: FedFormer通过上下文感知的注意力聚合机制，有效提升了联邦强化学习的性能和扩展性，同时满足隐私保护要求。

authors_text: Subhojyoti Mukherjee
title: Safety Aware Changepoint Detection for Piecewise i.i.d. Bandits
title_zh: 分段 i.i.d. 的安全感知变点检测土匪
comment: Accepted for the 38th Conference on Uncertainty in Artificial Intelligence (UAI 2022)
comments: Accepted for the 38th Conference on Uncertainty in Artificial Intelligence (UAI 2022)
abstract: In this paper, we consider the setting of piecewise i.i.d. bandits under a safety constraint. In this piecewise i.i.d. setting, there exists a finite number of changepoints where the mean of some or all arms change simultaneously. We introduce the safety constraint studied in \citet{wu2016conservative} to this setting such that at any round the cumulative reward is above a constant factor of the default action reward. We propose two actively adaptive algorithms for this setting that satisfy the safety constraint, detect changepoints, and restart without the knowledge of the number of changepoints or their locations. We provide regret bounds for our algorithms and show that the bounds are comparable to their counterparts from the safe bandit and piecewise i.i.d. bandit literature. We also provide the first matching lower bounds for this setting. Empirically, we show that our safety-aware algorithms perform similarly to the state-of-the-art actively adaptive algorithms that do not satisfy the safety constraint.
summary: In this paper, we consider the setting of piecewise i.i.d. bandits under a safety constraint. In this piecewise i.i.d. setting, there exists a finite number of changepoints where the mean of some or all arms change simultaneously. We introduce the safety constraint studied in \citet{wu2016conservative} to this setting such that at any round the cumulative reward is above a constant factor of the default action reward. We propose two actively adaptive algorithms for this setting that satisfy the safety constraint, detect changepoints, and restart without the knowledge of the number of changepoints or their locations. We provide regret bounds for our algorithms and show that the bounds are comparable to their counterparts from the safe bandit and piecewise i.i.d. bandit literature. We also provide the first matching lower bounds for this setting. Empirically, we show that our safety-aware algorithms perform similarly to the state-of-the-art actively adaptive algorithms that do not satisfy the safety constraint.
abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们考虑分段独立同分布的设置。安全约束下的强盗。在这个分段 i.i.d.设置中，存在有限数量的变化点，其中一些或所有臂的平均值同时变化。我们将 \citet{wu2016conservative} 中研究的安全约束引入到此设置中，以便在任何一轮中累积奖励都高于默认操作奖励的常数因子。我们为此设置提出了两种主动自适应算法，它们满足安全约束、检测变化点并在不知道变化点的数量或其位置的情况下重新启动。我们为我们的算法提供了遗憾界限，并表明该界限与安全老虎机和分段独立同分布的对应界限相当。强盗文学。我们还为此设置提供第一个匹配的下限。根据经验，我们表明我们的安全感知算法的性能与不满足安全约束的最先进的主动自适应算法类似。

AI.tldr: 本文研究了在安全约束下的分段独立同分布（piecewise i.i.d.）老虎机问题，提出两种能够检测突变点并满足安全约束的自适应算法。
AI.motivation: 在动态环境中，传统老虎机算法可能因突变点导致性能下降，且缺乏对安全性的保障。现有方法或无法满足安全约束，或无法有效检测突变点。
AI.method: 引入安全约束条件，确保累计奖励不低于默认动作奖励的常数倍；设计两种无需预知突变点数量和位置的自适应算法，结合突变点检测与安全约束机制。
AI.result: 理论分析给出与安全老虎机和分段i.i.d.老虎机文献相当的遗憾界，并首次建立该场景的下界；实验显示安全算法性能接近无安全约束的最先进方法。
AI.conclusion: 本文将安全约束与突变点检测结合，为动态安全强化学习提供了理论保证和有效算法，平衡了安全性与自适应性需求。

search_text: Safety Aware Changepoint Detection for Piecewise i.i.d. Bandits 分段 i.i.d. 的安全感知变点检测土匪 In this paper, we consider the setting of piecewise i.i.d. bandits under a safety constraint. In this piecewise i.i.d. setting, there exists a finite number of changepoints where the mean of some or all arms change simultaneously. We introduce the safety constraint studied in \citet{wu2016conservative} to this setting such that at any round the cumulative reward is above a constant factor of the default action reward. We propose two actively adaptive algorithms for this setting that satisfy the safety constraint, detect changepoints, and restart without the knowledge of the number of changepoints or their locations. We provide regret bounds for our algorithms and show that the bounds are comparable to their counterparts from the safe bandit and piecewise i.i.d. bandit literature. We also provide the first matching lower bounds for this setting. Empirically, we show that our safety-aware algorithms perform similarly to the state-of-the-art actively adaptive algorithms that do not satisfy the safety constraint. 在本文中，我们考虑分段独立同分布的设置。安全约束下的强盗。在这个分段 i.i.d.设置中，存在有限数量的变化点，其中一些或所有臂的平均值同时变化。我们将 \citet{wu2016conservative} 中研究的安全约束引入到此设置中，以便在任何一轮中累积奖励都高于默认操作奖励的常数因子。我们为此设置提出了两种主动自适应算法，它们满足安全约束、检测变化点并在不知道变化点的数量或其位置的情况下重新启动。我们为我们的算法提供了遗憾界限，并表明该界限与安全老虎机和分段独立同分布的对应界限相当。强盗文学。我们还为此设置提供第一个匹配的下限。根据经验，我们表明我们的安全感知算法的性能与不满足安全约束的最先进的主动自适应算法类似。 本文研究了在安全约束下的分段独立同分布（piecewise i.i.d.）老虎机问题，提出两种能够检测突变点并满足安全约束的自适应算法。 Subhojyoti Mukherjee cs.LG,cs.AI

authors_text: Jiahe Lan,Rui Zhang,Zheng Yan,Jie Wang,Yu Chen,Ronghui Hou
title: Adversarial attacks and defenses in Speaker Recognition Systems: A survey
title_zh: 说话人识别系统中的对抗性攻击和防御：一项调查
comment: 38pages, 2 figures, 2 tables. Journal of Systems Architecture,2022
comments: 38pages, 2 figures, 2 tables. Journal of Systems Architecture,2022
abstract: Speaker recognition has become very popular in many application scenarios, such as smart homes and smart assistants, due to ease of use for remote control and economic-friendly features. The rapid development of SRSs is inseparable from the advancement of machine learning, especially neural networks. However, previous work has shown that machine learning models are vulnerable to adversarial attacks in the image domain, which inspired researchers to explore adversarial attacks and defenses in Speaker Recognition Systems (SRS). Unfortunately, existing literature lacks a thorough review of this topic. In this paper, we fill this gap by performing a comprehensive survey on adversarial attacks and defenses in SRSs. We first introduce the basics of SRSs and concepts related to adversarial attacks. Then, we propose two sets of criteria to evaluate the performance of attack methods and defense methods in SRSs, respectively. After that, we provide taxonomies of existing attack methods and defense methods, and further review them by employing our proposed criteria. Finally, based on our review, we find some open issues and further specify a number of future directions to motivate the research of SRSs security.

We first introduce the basics of SRSs and concepts related to adversarial attacks. After that, we provide taxonomies of existing attack methods and defense methods, and further review them by employing our proposed criteria. Finally, based on our review, we find some open issues and further specify a number of future directions to motivate the research of SRSs security.
abstract_zh: 由于易于远程控制和经济友好的特点，说话人识别在智能家居和智能助理等许多应用场景中变得非常流行。 SRS的快速发展与机器学习尤其是神经网络的进步密不可分。然而，之前的工作表明，机器学习模型容易受到图像领域的对抗性攻击，这激发了研究人员探索说话人识别系统（SRS）中的对抗性攻击和防御。不幸的是，现有文献缺乏对此主题的彻底回顾。在本文中，我们通过对 SRS 中的对抗性攻击和防御进行全面调查来填补这一空白。我们首先介绍 SRS 的基础知识和与对抗性攻击相关的概念。然后，我们提出了两组标准来分别评估SRS中攻击方法和防御方法的性能。之后，我们提供现有攻击方法和防御方法的分类，并使用我们提出的标准进一步审查它们。最后，根据我们的审查，我们发现了一些悬而未决的问题，并进一步明确了一些未来的方向，以推动 SRS 安全性的研究。

AI.tldr: 本文对说话人识别系统中的对抗攻击与防御技术进行全面综述，并提出评估标准与未来研究方向。
AI.motivation: 现有研究缺乏对说话人识别系统对抗攻击与防御的系统性综述，需填补这一空白以提升系统安全性。
AI.method: 提出攻击与防御方法的评估标准，分类现有方法并基于标准进行系统性分析。
AI.result: 识别出现有方法的不足，明确未来研究方向，如更鲁棒的防御机制与跨领域攻击研究。
AI.conclusion: 需进一步研究更有效的防御策略及应对新型对抗攻击的解决方案，以增强说话人识别系统的安全性。

authors_text: Dmitry Petrov,Matheus Gadelha,Radomir Mech,Evangelos Kalogerakis
title: ANISE: Assembly-based Neural Implicit Surface rEconstruction
title_zh: ANISE：基于装配的神经隐式表面重建
abstract: We present ANISE, a method that reconstructs a 3D~shape from partial observations (images or sparse point clouds) using a part-aware neural implicit shape representation. The shape is formulated as an assembly of neural implicit functions, each representing a different part instance. In contrast to previous approaches, the prediction of this representation proceeds in a coarse-to-fine manner. Our model first reconstructs a structural arrangement of the shape in the form of geometric transformations of its part instances. Conditioned on them, the model predicts part latent codes encoding their surface geometry. Reconstructions can be obtained in two ways: (i) by directly decoding the part latent codes to part implicit functions, then combining them into the final shape; or (ii) by using part latents to retrieve similar part instances in a part database and assembling them in a single shape. We demonstrate that, when performing reconstruction by decoding part representations into implicit functions, our method achieves state-of-the-art part-aware reconstruction results from both images and sparse point clouds.When reconstructing shapes by assembling parts retrieved from a dataset, our approach significantly outperforms traditional shape retrieval methods even when significantly restricting the database size. We present our results in well-known sparse point cloud reconstruction and single-view reconstruction benchmarks.
summary: We present ANISE, a method that reconstructs a 3D~shape from partial observations (images or sparse point clouds) using a part-aware neural implicit shape representation. The shape is formulated as an assembly of neural implicit functions, each representing a different part instance. In contrast to previous approaches, the prediction of this representation proceeds in a coarse-to-fine manner. Our model first reconstructs a structural arrangement of the shape in the form of geometric transformations of its part instances. Conditioned on them, the model predicts part latent codes encoding their surface geometry. Reconstructions can be obtained in two ways: (i) by directly decoding the part latent codes to part implicit functions, then combining them into the final shape; or (ii) by using part latents to retrieve similar part instances in a part database and assembling them in a single shape. We demonstrate that, when performing reconstruction by decoding part representations into implicit functions, our method achieves state-of-the-art part-aware reconstruction results from both images and sparse point clouds.When reconstructing shapes by assembling parts retrieved from a dataset, our approach significantly outperforms traditional shape retrieval methods even when significantly restricting the database size. We present our results in well-known sparse point cloud reconstruction and single-view reconstruction benchmarks.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了 ANISE，一种使用部分感知神经隐式形状表示从部分观察（图像或稀疏点云）重建 3D 形状的方法。该形状被表述为神经隐式函数的集合，每个函数代表不同的零件实例。与以前的方法相比，这种表示的预测以从粗到细的方式进行。我们的模型首先以其零件实例的几何变换的形式重建形状的结构排列。以它们为条件，模型预测编码其表面几何形状的零件潜在代码。重构可以通过两种方式获得：（i）直接将部分潜码解码为部分隐函数，然后将它们组合成最终形状；或者（ii）通过使用零件潜在信息来检索零件数据库中的相似零件实例并将它们组装成单个形状。我们证明，当通过将零件表示解码为隐式函数来执行重建时，我们的方法从图像和稀疏点云中实现了最先进的零件感知重建结果。当通过组装从数据集中检索到的零件来重建形状时，即使在显着限制数据库大小的情况下，我们的方法也显着优于传统的形状检索方法。我们在著名的稀疏点云重建和单视图重建基准中展示了我们的结果。

AI.tldr: 本文提出ANISE方法，通过部分感知的神经隐式形状表示，从图像或稀疏点云等部分观测数据中重建3D形状。
AI.motivation: 传统方法在处理复杂形状的局部细节和结构装配时存在局限，需通过分阶段策略提升重建精度与鲁棒性。
AI.method: 将形状建模为神经隐式函数的集合，采用粗到细策略：先通过几何变换预测结构布局，再解码部分潜在码生成隐式函数或检索数据库中相似部件进行装配。
AI.result: 在稀疏点云和单视角重建基准测试中，ANISE在部分感知重建和检索装配任务中均达到或超越现有方法性能，尤其在小规模数据库下优势显著。
AI.conclusion: ANISE通过分阶段隐式表示与部件装配策略，有效提升了复杂形状的重建质量，为基于检索的3D重建提供了新思路。
